content: For PROC-04, list the ordered handoffs as STEP-A->STEP-B tokens, comma-separated.

content.content:
{"content": "Subject: match exceptions
On STEP-44 the tolerance % is just whatever AP has always done, not written
anywhere. If it's out of tolerance we kick it to STEP-45 and the reason only
lives in this email chain - there's no system field for it. (STEP-49 slotting is
a merch-promo thing, different flow - ignore it here.)", "kind": "email_thread", "process_id": "PROC-04", "source_id": "SRC-401"}

{"kind": "spreadsheet", "process_id": "PROC-04", "rows": [["step_code", "note"], ["STEP-41", "dock counts still come in on the clipboard, keyed in that night"], ["STEP-43", "PO in CASES, ASN in EACHES, invoice by WEIGHT - pack math never reconciled"]], "source_id": "SRC-402"}

{"content": "Portal path:
Screen 1 -> EDI inbox: STEP-40 ASN (856) lands structured.
Screen 3 -> match panel: STEP-42 compares receipt/PO/invoice when all three are in.
Screen 7 -> STEP-46 posts the receipt and accrues.", "kind": "portal_clickpath", "process_id": "PROC-04", "source_id": "SRC-403"}

{"content": "order of ops: STEP-40 -> STEP-41 -> STEP-42 -> STEP-43 -> STEP-44 -> STEP-45 ->\nSTEP-46. dock count -> match -> fix the UOM -> tolerance -> if it fails, route by\nemail, then post. half of this only makes sense if you've done it a year.", "kind": "tribal_note", "process_id": "PROC-04", "source_id": "SRC-404"}
